mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and the Federal Funds Rate is rising, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening to combat price pressures. This dual pressure increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% MoM
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts indicate strong consumer and business confidence, driving demand for related goods and services. This positive economic activity supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows sustained growth during an economic expansion, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand and potential economic slowdown. This directly impacts corporate revenues and profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS falls by more than 1.0% MoM, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals a weakening manufacturing sector and overall supply-side distress. This translates to lower corporate output and earnings, leading to immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high federal funds rates indicate a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers. This dampens economic activity and corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and low unemployment indicates a robust and healthy economy. This environment fosters strong corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is above 2.0% YoY and UNRATE is below 5.0%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.
